1 ## Instructions
    2 (No stored memories found in GEMINI.md or MEMORY.md files.)
    3
    4 ## Identity
    5 [2026-04-29] - Name: Yash Singh.
    6 [2026-04-29] - Education: IIIT Una (B.Tech CS, GPA 8.3, graduating July 2026).
    7 [2026-05-09] - Phone: +91 8755-765-125.
    8 [unknown] - Portfolio: https://itsyash.space.
    9 [unknown] - LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/yash-singh-2757aa1b4/.
   10 [unknown] - GitHub: https://github.com/yashs33244.
   11 [unknown] - Resume: https://drive.google.com/file/d/117KGPqnxCYgZ-tvHkL65yx9cbG3jv5AZ/view?usp=drivesdk.
   12
   13 ## Career
   14 [2026-04-29] - Current Role: Software Development Engineer at Binocs, Bangalore (July 2025–Present).
   15 [2026-04-29] - General skill areas: React/Next.js, FastAPI/Node.js, AWS + Kubernetes, LLM automation, DevOps, Full-stack engineering.
   16 [2026-04-29] - Expertise: "I build and ship full-stack products end-to-end, fast."
   17 [2026-05-09] - Flexiblity: "highly flexible across DevOps, frontend, and backend engineering."
   18
   19 ## Projects
   20 [2026-04-29] - Binocs AI Pipeline: Redesigned with prompt chaining and structured outputs, reducing Claude API cost by 99% ($1.00 to $0.01 per slide); now the company-wide standard.
   21 [2026-04-29] - Production AI Agents: Shipped 5+ production AI agents with human-in-the-loop orchestration, cutting task completion from 3–4 engineer-days to 1 day.
   22 [2026-04-29] - Admin Dashboard: Launched a full-stack dashboard in 24 hours using React, FastAPI, Terraform-provisioned AWS, and Kubernetes deployment.
   23 [2026-04-29] - Resume Builder Platform: Built platform (Next.js + PostgreSQL + Docker) serving 1,000+ monthly users at 99.9% uptime.
   24
   25 ## Preferences
   26 [2026-04-29] - Outreach style: informal "tldr;" style; removed "Job link" and "work sample / recent project" asks from email bodies.
   27 [2026-05-09] - Working style: "I love building. I’m not great at pretending I have a million hobbies. I like taking something from 0 -> 1 (or 1 ->100) and being heads-down chasing that
      goal."
   28 [2026-05-09] - Working style: "I enjoy building real systems end-to-end whether that means infrastructure, deployment, backend services, or user-facing products."
   29 [2026-05-09] - Career preference: "I’m excited by environments where I can learn fast, contribute broadly, and create meaningful impact through execution."
   30 [2026-05-09] - Tool usage: "be optimistic with apollo api credits." This folder has batch six and most of the emails in batch six are not populated with Apollo API. In somewhere in these files in the Indian outreach folder, the Apollo API key was there, like in the root folder of the current folder. There is a.env and I've pasted the Apollo API key there so use that. What you have to do is get that company's HR or engineering manager or some type of manager from the hiring team or tag team. Just get their email, only one email. Be optimistic with the Apollo API key. Do not burn credits. Be optimistic and populate the emails in the Bad6

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.

file.content:
import openpyxl
import requests
import os
import time
from dotenv import load_dotenv

# Load environment variables
load_dotenv()
APOLLO_API_KEY = os.getenv("APOLLO_API_KEY")
XLSX_PATH = "indian_startup_outreach/batch_6_ready.xlsx"

# Titles to search for, in order of priority
TARGET_TITLES = [
    "Engineering Manager",
    "Technical Lead",
    "Tech Lead",
    "Head of Engineering",
    "CTO",
    "Talent Acquisition",
    "HR Manager",
    "Human Resources",
    "Recruiter"
]

def search_apollo(company_name):
    search_url = "https://api.apollo.io/v1/mixed_people/api_search"
    match_url = "https://api.apollo.io/v1/people/match"
    
    headers = {
        'Cache-Control': 'no-cache',
        'Content-Type': 'application/json',
        'X-Api-Key': APOLLO_API_KEY
    }
    
    search_data = {
        "q_organization_name": company_name,
        "person_titles": TARGET_TITLES,
        "page": 1
    }
    
    try:
        # Step 1: Search for people
        response = requests.post(search_url, headers=headers, json=search_data)
        if response.status_code == 200:
            results = response.json()
            people = results.get('people', [])
            if not people:
                return None
            
            # Step 2: Pick the best person and match to get email
            best_person = None
            for person in people:
                org_name = person.get('organization', {}).get('name', '').lower()
                if company_name.lower() in org_name or org_name in company_name.lower():
                    best_person = person
                    break
            
            if not best_person:
                best_person = people[0]
            
            # Step 3: Match/Enrich to get email
            match_data = {"id": best_person['id']}
            match_res = requests.post(match_url, headers=headers, json=match_data)
            if match_res.status_code == 200:
                enriched_person = match_res.json().get('person', {})
                return {
                    "name": enriched_person.get('name'),
                    "first_name": enriched_person.get('first_name'),
                    "title": enriched_person.get('title'),
                    "email": enriched_person.get('email')
                }
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error searching {company_name}: {e}")
    return None

def main():
    if not os.path.exists(XLSX_PATH):
        print(f"Error: {XLSX_PATH} not found.")
        return

    wb = openpyxl.load_workbook(XLSX_PATH)
    ws = wb.active
    
    headers = [ws.cell(1, c).value for c in range(1, ws.max_column + 1)]
    company_idx = headers.index('company') + 1
    email_idx = headers.index('email') + 1
    cover_idx = headers.index('cover_letter') + 1
    
    if 'contact_name' not in headers:
        ws.cell(row=1, column=ws.max_column + 1, value='contact_name')
        ws.cell(row=1, column=ws.max_column + 1, value='contact_title')
        headers = [ws.cell(1, c).value for c in range(1, ws.max_column + 1)]
    
    name_idx = headers.index('contact_name') + 1
    title_idx = headers.index('contact_title') + 1

    total_rows = ws.max_row - 1
    processed = 0
    enriched = 0

    print(f"Starting enrichment for {total_rows} companies...")

    for r in range(2, ws.max_row + 1):
        company = ws.cell(r, company_idx).value
        contact_existing = ws.cell(r, name_idx).value
        
        if not company or contact_existing:
            continue
            
        print(f"[{r-1}/{total_rows}] Searching for {company}...")
        contact = search_apollo(company)
        
        if contact:
            if contact['email']:
                ws.cell(r, email_idx).value = contact['email']
                enriched += 1
                print(f"  Found: {contact['name']} ({contact['title']}) - {contact['email']}")
            else:
                print(f"  Found contact but no email: {contact['name']} ({contact['title']})")
            
            ws.cell(r, name_idx).value = contact['name']
            ws.cell(r, title_idx).value = contact['title']
            
            # Personalize cover letter
            cl = ws.cell(r, cover_idx).value
            if cl and contact['first_name'] and (f"Hi {company} Team," in cl or f"Hi {company} Team,".lower() in cl.lower()):
                # Case insensitive replace for "Hi {company} Team,"
                pattern = f"Hi {company} Team,"
                if pattern in cl:
                    ws.cell(r, cover_idx).value = cl.replace(pattern, f"Hi {contact['first_name']},")
                else:
                    # Try to find it even if case differs slightly
                    ws.cell(r, cover_idx).value = cl.replace(f"Hi {company} Team,", f"Hi {contact['first_name']},")
        else:
            print(f"  No contact found.")
            
        processed += 1
        if processed % 5 == 0:
            wb.save(XLSX_PATH)
            print(f"  Progress saved...")
        
        time.sleep(1) # Rate limit

    wb.save(XLSX_PATH)
    print(f"\nEnrichment complete! Total processed: {processed}, Total enriched with emails: {enriched}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import openpyxl
wb = openpyxl.load_workbook('indian_startup_outreach/batch_6_ready.xlsx')
ws = wb.active
headers = [ws.cell(1, c).value for c in range(1, ws.max_column + 1)]
email_idx = headers.index('email') + 1
name_idx = headers.index('contact_name') + 1
status_idx = headers.index('status') + 1

needs_email = []
for r in range(2, ws.max_row + 1):
    company = ws.cell(r, 1).value
    email = ws.cell(r, email_idx).value
    contact = ws.cell(r, name_idx).value
    status = ws.cell(r, status_idx).value
    if not email and company:
        needs_email.append((r, company, status))

print(f'Rows needing email: {len(needs_email)}')
for row in needs_email[:20]:
    print(row)
"

import openpyxl
wb = openpyxl.load_workbook('indian_startup_outreach/batch_6_ready.xlsx')
ws = wb.active
headers = [ws.cell(1, c).value for c in range(1, ws.max_column + 1)]
email_idx = headers.index('email') + 1
name_idx = headers.index('contact_name') + 1
status_idx = headers.index('status') + 1

# Check generic emails (careers@, info@, etc.) without a personal contact name
generic_emails = []
for r in range(2, ws.max_row + 1):
    company = ws.cell(r, 1).value
    email = ws.cell(r, email_idx).value
    contact = ws.cell(r, name_idx).value
    status = ws.cell(r, status_idx).value
    if email and not contact and company:
        generic_emails.append((r, company, email, status))

print(f'Rows with email but no contact name: {len(generic_emails)}')
for row in generic_emails[:30]:
    print(row)
"

import openpyxl
wb = openpyxl.load_workbook('indian_startup_outreach/batch_6_ready.xlsx')
ws = wb.active
headers = [ws.cell(1, c).value for c in range(1, ws.max_column + 1)]
email_idx = headers.index('email') + 1
name_idx = headers.index('contact_name') + 1
status_idx = headers.index('status') + 1

new_rows = []
for r in range(2, ws.max_row + 1):
    company = ws.cell(r, 1).value
    email = ws.cell(r, email_idx).value
    contact = ws.cell(r, name_idx).value
    status = ws.cell(r, status_idx).value
    if status == 'NEW' and company:
        new_rows.append((r, company, email, contact))

print(f'NEW status rows: {len(new_rows)}')
# Find NEW rows with generic or missing email and no contact
to_enrich = [(r, c, e) for r, c, e, n in new_rows if not n]
print(f'NEW rows needing Apollo enrichment: {len(to_enrich)}')
for row in to_enrich[:40]:
    print(row)
"

    company = ws.cell(r, 1).value
    email = ws.cell(r, email_idx).value
    status = ws.cell(r, status_idx).value
    if status == 'NEW' and not email and company:
        no_email.append((r, company))

print(f'NEW rows with NO email: {len(no_email)}')
for row in no_email:
    print(row)
